user_query:
We're curating plain-language, dyslexia-friendly summaries for our community resource library. Please use the provided inputs to create translated (to English, when needed) and simplified versions, while tracking success/failure from a prior CLI run log.

Inputs:
- input/resources.csv
- logs/translate_run.log

What to do:
1) Determine which resources are processable by analyzing logs/translate_run.log:
   - Treat a line with "[SUCCESS]" for a given id as a successful translation run for that id.
   - Treat a line that starts with "ERROR id=<n>" as a failed run for that id and capture the error message (e.g., "network timeout").
   - If both SUCCESS and ERROR appear for the same id, use the last occurrence in the log to decide final status.
   - Only process ids that:
     a) exist in input/resources.csv,
     b) have final status SUCCESS in the log,
     c) are not tagged with the tag "internal" (exclude those entirely from processing).
   - Record any ids that appear in the log but are missing from the CSV as orphan_log_ids, and any CSV ids that never appear in the log as missing_in_log_ids.

2) For each processable record:
   - If lang != "en": translate text to English (you can use your own method) and set was_translated=true and source_lang to the original language.
   - If lang == "en": set was_translated=false and source_lang="en".
   - Simplify the English text for dyslexic readers using short sentences and commonly-used words. Keep it as plain text.
   - Compute readability metrics before and after simplification using the following deterministic rules:
     • Sentence segmentation: split on '.', '!' or '?' characters; ignore empty segments.
     • Word count: split on whitespace.
     • avg_words_per_sentence_before = (original_word_count / original_sentence_count)
     • avg_words_per_sentence_after = (simplified_word_count / simplified_sentence_count)
     • improvement_score = avg_words_per_sentence_before - avg_words_per_sentence_after (higher is better)

3) Filtering, sorting, and ranking:
   - Only include processable records in the main output.
   - Create a ranking of all processed records by improvement_score (descending). Break ties by ascending id.
   - Export the top 3 entries by improvement_score to a separate CSV.

4) Write outputs to local files:
   - output/simplified_resources.jsonl: one JSON object per line with fields:
     { id, title, original_lang, source_lang, was_translated, original_sentence_count, original_word_count, avg_words_per_sentence_before, simplified_sentence_count, simplified_word_count, avg_words_per_sentence_after, improvement_score, simplified_text }
   - output/top_improvements.csv: columns = id,title,improvement_score; include the top 3 by improvement_score.
   - output/summary.json: a summary with fields:
     {
       resources_in_csv,
       ids_in_log,
       processed_ids,
       error_ids,
       missing_in_log_ids,
       orphan_log_ids,
       excluded_by_tag_ids,
       error_type_counts (map of error message -> count)
     }
   - The counts and id lists should be consistent with both input/resources.csv and logs/translate_run.log.

Success criteria:
- The number of processed_ids equals the number of SUCCESS ids that exist in the CSV and are not tagged "internal".
- No ERROR or missing_in_log items appear in output/simplified_resources.jsonl or output/top_improvements.csv.
- The ranking in output/top_improvements.csv matches the improvement_score sort order and the tie-break rule.
- All three outputs are created under the output/ directory.

Notes:
- Keep translations and simplifications in English. You do not need perfect literary quality—focus on producing shorter sentences. Avoid removing essential meaning.
- Do not modify the inputs.
- Make sure all required fields are present in each output record.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
